steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause: “the portraits reveal themselves to be quilts”. After the blank is a noun phrase with a participial modifier: “the stitching barely visible among...”. This phrase contains no main verb, so it is not an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A comma separates an independent clause from a following noun phrase that provides additional information.

Choices C and D use punctuation designed for independent clauses, while choice A adds “and,” which illogically suggests quilts and stitching are two equal things.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
